user_query:
I need a link audit across two documents before our launch. Please do the following using the files under input/ and write all outputs under output/:

1) Extract every http/https URL from both input/site_pages.md and input/landing.html, deduplicate them, sort lexicographically, and save the list to output/urls.json as a JSON array of strings.

2) Check each URL with an HTTP HEAD request, following redirects. Use a 5-second connection timeout and 1 retry. Categorize each result into one of: OK (2xx), REDIRECT (3xx), CLIENT_ERROR (4xx), SERVER_ERROR (5xx), TIMEOUT, or ERROR.

3) Save structured results to:
   - output/checks.json as an array of objects with fields: url (string), status_code (string), status_label (one of the 6 categories above), checked_at (ISO 8601 timestamp).
   - output/checks.csv with the exact header: url,status_code,status_label,checked_at and one row per URL.

4) Create output/broken-links.md titled "Broken Links Triage" that groups non-OK results under these sections: 4xx, 5xx, Timeout/Error, and Redirects (3xx). For each group, list affected URLs and propose concrete remediation steps (e.g., update/remove link, replace with alternative, fix server-side issue, update to final target), include an owner and suggested timeframe. Include general remediation guidance for each section even if no URL falls into that category. At minimum, include concrete suggestions referencing representative cases from the inputs that commonly exhibit 404, 500, and DNS/timeout failures. Start the document with a one-paragraph summary that states the total unique URL count and a brief risk assessment.

Notes:
- Read only input/site_pages.md and input/landing.html for URL discovery.
- Ensure every URL extracted appears in both output/urls.json and the two results files.
- The status labels must be exactly one of: OK, REDIRECT, CLIENT_ERROR, SERVER_ERROR, TIMEOUT, ERROR.
- All outputs must be written to the specified paths under output/.
